text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY — BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE
ANNUAL POST-TRANSPLANT SURVEILLANCE (DAY +365)

PATIENT: Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo
SEX: Female
AGE: 49 years
CLINICAL INDICATION: AML post-allogeneic HSCT (MUD, 10/10 HLA-matched, FluBu4 myeloablative conditioning), day +365 annual surveillance. Known history: AML, NOS (FAB M1), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (0.91), NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8, DNMT3A R882H, TET2 Q1299*, WT1 R462W, NRAS Q61H. ELN 2022 Adverse risk. Prior CR1 MRD-negative documented at day +35, +30, +180, and +365 time points.
SUBMITTING CLINICIAN: Hematology-Oncology / Allogeneic Stem Cell Transplant Service

─────────────────────────────────────────
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────
Specimen ID: [LAB-ID-REDACTED]
Date of Procedure: [DATE REDACTED — TO BE POPULATED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
Type of Specimen:
  A. Bone marrow core biopsy (right posterior iliac crest)
  B. Bone marrow aspirate (right posterior iliac crest, same site)
  C. Peripheral blood (submitted concurrently for chimerism and molecular studies)

Procedure performed by: Hematology-Oncology fellow under attending supervision
Adequacy: Adequate. Core biopsy length: 2.1 cm (15 inter-trabecular spaces evaluable). Aspirate: multiple spicule-containing pulls obtained; 300+ cells counted for differential.

─────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS PATHOLOGY
─────────────────────────────────────────
A. Core biopsy: One core of tan-pink trabecular bone and marrow tissue measuring 2.1 x 0.2 cm. Submitted entirely in one cassette, decalcified, processed for hematoxylin and eosin (H&E), Giemsa, reticulin (silver), and CD34 immunohistochemical staining.

B. Aspirate smears: Multiple air-dried Wright-Giemsa-stained smears and clot section prepared. Clot section embedded and processed in paraffin. Smears submitted for morphologic differential. Additional aspirate material submitted in appropriate transport media for flow cytometry immunophenotyping and molecular studies.

C. Peripheral blood: EDTA anticoagulated tube submitted for STR-based chimerism analysis and molecular MRD studies.

─────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
─────────────────────────────────────────

I. MORPHOLOGY

Core Biopsy (H&E and Giemsa):
The bone marrow core biopsy is normocellular with an estimated overall cellularity of 45–50%, appropriate for a 49-year-old female. The bony trabeculae appear unremarkable without osteosclerosis, necrosis, or vascular abnormalities. Interstitial fat is present in normal proportions.

Hematopoiesis is trilineage, morphologically mature, and well-represented. Erythroid islands are distributed throughout the marrow with predominance of polychromatophilic and orthochromatic normoblasts, indicating normal late-stage erythropoiesis. Myeloid elements show full maturation through bands and segmented neutrophils; no left-shift or dysplastic forms identified. Megakaryocytes are present in adequate numbers (2–4 per high-power field), distributed evenly, and display normal lobulated nuclear morphology without dysplastic clustering, nuclear hypolobulation, or emperipolesis.

Importantly, there is NO morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia. Blasts are rare and do not exceed 2% of the total nucleated cells. No Auer rods are identified. No sheets, clusters, or aggregates of immature cells are seen.

No lymphoid aggregates, granulomas, plasma cell expansions, or stromal abnormalities are identified.

Reticulin stain: Grade MF-0 (no reticulin fibrosis; no increase over normal). No collagen fibrosis.

CD34 immunohistochemistry: CD34-positive cells (endothelial cells and rare blasts) are present only in perivascular locations with no increase in interstitial CD34+ blasts. CD34+ blast count by IHC: less than 1% of total nucleated cells.

Aspiratemorphology (Wright-Giemsa smears and clot section):
300-cell manual differential performed on spicule-containing smears:
  Myeloblasts:       1.8%
  Promyelocytes:     3.0%
  Myelocytes:        10.2%
  Metamyelocytes:    9.5%
  Bands:             14.1%
  Segmented PMNs:    22.4%
  Eosinophils:       2.7%
  Basophils:         0.5%
  Monocytes:         4.3%
  Erythroid precursors (all stages): 23.6%
  Lymphocytes:       7.6%
  Plasma cells:      0.3%
  M:E ratio: approximately 2.8:1 (within normal limits)

Blast morphology: The rare blasts present are small-to-intermediate in size with round-to-slightly irregular nuclei, inconspicuous nucleoli, and scant cytoplasm — morphologically consistent with normal hematopoietic precursors. NO large granular blasts, NO Auer rods, NO bilobed or hypogranular monocytic forms identified. The appearance is entirely inconsistent with leukemic blast morphology.

Dysplasia assessment: No significant dysplasia is identified in any lineage. Specifically, erythroid dysplasia (nuclear bridging, karyorrhexis, nuclear irregularity) is absent or minimal (less than 10% of erythroid cells). Myeloid dysplasia (hypogranulation, pseudo-Pelger-Huet) absent. Megakaryocytic dysplasia absent.

─────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
─────────────────────────────────────────

II. FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING — MINIMAL RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD) ASSAY
Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate
Assay: 10-color multiparameter flow cytometry, minimum 300,000 viable events acquired
Sensitivity: 0.01% (1 in 10,000 viable cells)

Method: Standard LAIP (leukemia-associated immunophenotype)-based MRD detection using the known aberrant blast phenotype established at diagnosis (CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/CD123+/HLA-DR+/MPO+ with asynchronous antigen expression). DfN (different from normal) approach applied as adjunct.

Result: NEGATIVE — NO ABERRANT BLAST POPULATION DETECTED.

Comment: No population expressing the leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) from the index diagnosis is identified. The CD34-positive compartment consists only of normal residual hematopoietic progenitors (approximately 0.08% of viable events) without aberrant antigen co-expression. CD117 (c-Kit)-positive mast cells are present in normal numbers without immunophenotypic aberrancy. CD33/CD13 co-expression patterns are within the normal myeloid maturation spectrum. No evidence of CD34+/CD123+ blast excess. No monocytic population with aberrant antigen loss or gain. MRD result: NEGATIVE (less than 0.01% threshold).

III. MOLECULAR STUDIES — MRD BY POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION
Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate (FLT3-ITD, WT1) and peripheral blood (all molecular markers)

a) FLT3-ITD Allele-Specific PCR (bone marrow aspirate)
Assay: Capillary electrophoresis-based fragment analysis, sensitivity approximately 10^-4 (0.01%)
Target: 33-base pair insertion in FLT3 exon 14 (patient-specific ITD identified at diagnosis, VAF 48.2%)
Result: NOT DETECTED — FLT3-ITD mutant allele is below the limit of detection.
Comment: No amplification product of appropriate size for the 33-bp FLT3-ITD is observed above the sensitivity threshold. Internal control amplification confirmed (wild-type FLT3 allele detected). This result is consistent with molecular remission by FLT3-ITD PCR.

b) WT1 Quantitative RT-PCR (peripheral blood)
Assay: EAC (Europe Against Cancer) standardized WT1 quantitative RT-PCR; results expressed as WT1 copies per 10^4 ABL1 copies
Result: 9 copies/10^4 ABL1 copies
Reference range (this laboratory): Less than 50 copies/10^4 ABL1 (peripheral blood)
Comment: WT1 transcript level is within the normal reference range. Serial comparison:
  Diagnosis (bone marrow): markedly elevated (not quantified, qualitatively very high)
  Day +35 bone marrow: 26 copies/10^4 ABL1 (normal)
  Day +180 peripheral blood: 11 copies/10^4 ABL1 (normal)
  Day +365 peripheral blood: 9 copies/10^4 ABL1 (normal — present result)
Trend is reassuring, with stable low-normal WT1 transcript levels across all post-transplant time points. No evidence of WT1 transcript rising, which would raise concern for early molecular relapse.

c) DNMT3A p.R882H Allele-Specific PCR (peripheral blood)
Assay: Allele-specific quantitative PCR targeting the recurrent DNMT3A p.R882H substitution (c.2644C>T); sensitivity approximately 0.1% mutant allele fraction
Result: NOT DETECTED — mutant allele is below the limit of detection.
Comment: DNMT3A R882H was identified at diagnosis with a VAF of 49.8%, consistent with a likely founder or CHIP-origin mutation. Its clearance below the assay sensitivity threshold following allogeneic transplantation is consistent with successful engraftment of donor hematopoiesis and eradication of the host leukemic (and pre-leukemic) clone. This finding further supports complete donor engraftment and absence of residual host hematopoietic cells harboring this mutation.

IV. CHIMERISM ANALYSIS — SHORT TANDEM REPEAT (STR) PCR
Specimen: Peripheral blood (EDTA anticoagulated)
Method: Fluorescent capillary electrophoresis-based STR profiling; 16 polymorphic loci; sensitivity 1–5% for detection of minor populations.
Separated cell fractions analyzed: Unsorted whole leukocytes, CD3+ T-cell-enriched fraction, CD33+ myeloid-enriched fraction.

Results:
  Overall (unsorted peripheral blood chimerism): 99.9% DONOR (residual host: 0.1%, at lower margin of detectability)
  CD3+ T-cell fraction: 99.2% DONOR / 0.8% HOST
  CD33+ myeloid fraction: 100.0% DONOR (host alleles undetectable)

Serial chimerism comparison:
  Day +21: Overall 97%, CD3+ 89%, CD33+ 99%
  Day +30: Overall 99.4%, CD3+ 95.2%, CD33+ 100%
  Day +180: Overall 99.8%, CD3+ 98.1%, CD33+ 100%
  Day +365: Overall 99.9%, CD3+ 99.2%, CD33+ 100% (present results)

Comment: Full donor chimerism is sustained at day +365. Myeloid chimerism remains 100% donor throughout all post-transplant time points, which is the most relevant compartment for disease monitoring in AML. T-cell chimerism has progressively improved from 89% at day +21 to 99.2% at day +365, reflecting complete reconstitution of donor T-cell compartment. The minimal residual host signal in unsorted and CD3 fractions (less than 1%) is at the limit of assay detection and is not considered clinically significant; this may represent residual host NK or non-T lymphoid cells. There is no mixed chimerism pattern suggestive of host hematopoietic recovery or early leukemic relapse.

─────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY AND CONCLUSION
─────────────────────────────────────────

CLINICAL CONTEXT: 49-year-old female with a history of AML, NOS (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8, ELN 2022 Adverse risk), who achieved CR1 MRD-negative after 7+3 plus midostaurin induction and subsequently underwent myeloablative conditioning (FluBu4) allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplantation from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor. This specimen represents the day +365 annual surveillance bone marrow assessment.

CONCLUSION:

1. COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC REMISSION: Normocellular bone marrow (45–50% cellularity) with complete trilineage hematopoiesis. No morphologic evidence of AML. Myeloblasts less than 2%.

2. MRD NEGATIVE by multiparameter flow cytometry (sensitivity 0.01%, 10-color): No leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected.

3. FLT3-ITD PCR NEGATIVE (bone marrow aspirate): Mutant allele below limit of detection (sensitivity ~10^-4).

4. WT1 TRANSCRIPT LEVEL NORMAL: 9 copies/10^4 ABL1, well within the normal reference range and stable/declining across serial measurements.

5. DNMT3A R882H PCR NEGATIVE (peripheral blood): Founder-clone mutation undetectable, consistent with complete replacement of host hematopoiesis by donor graft.

6. FULL DONOR CHIMERISM: Overall 99.9% donor, myeloid compartment 100% donor, T-cell compartment 99.2% donor. No evidence of mixed chimerism or host hematopoietic reconstitution at day +365.

OVERALL ASSESSMENT: These findings are consistent with sustained complete remission with MRD negativity and full donor engraftment at 12 months post-allogeneic stem cell transplantation. All detectable molecular markers of the original AML clone — including FLT3-ITD and DNMT3A R882H (founder mutation) — are below assay detection limits. This represents a clinically favorable response trajectory.

SIGNED: [Attending Hematopathologist — signature redacted]
DIVISION OF HEMATOPATHOLOGY
[Institution Name Redacted]
